probableCauses.cause: Demand increase or inadequate lag support
evidence: Low pressure commonly appears when real demand exceeds available support.
Weak control response can hold pressure below setpoint without a hard alarm.

nextChecks.action: Capture steady-state discharge pressure versus setpoint.
Capture lead pump frequency while demand is stable.

escalationCriteria.condition: Pressure remains materially below setpoint after validation checks
escalationCriteria.action: Escalate to senior technician or engineer

closeOutNote: Technician validated low-pressure behavior and should record PSI, lead pump Hz, and whether demand, tuning, or restriction best explains the condition.

alternativePaths.name: Controls-first review
alternativePaths.description: If Hz is low while pressure is low, prioritize tuning and lag-threshold review.
alternativePaths.trigger: Low pressure paired with unexpectedly low Hz
